agent_outputs.raw: Defense wins. Split-before-augment with fold-local on-the-fly augmentation eliminates the contamination pathway. Original-image-level splits with physical-unit clustering prevent near-duplicate crossings. Later-week raw test provides clean evaluation. Scope is appropriately disclosed. VERDICT: defense_wins

The methodology is sound. The suspicious appearance — heavy augmentation of rare positives — is a red herring because the augmentation is confined to training folds by design. The evaluation pipeline produces a valid benchmark for the stated claim. VERDICT: defense_wins

The evaluation is valid. The fold-local augmentation policy is the critical design feature that prevents leakage. The later-week production test set using only raw images provides an additional, temporally separated evaluation. Scope disclosure is correct. No methodological issue. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: Unanimous defense wins across all three assessors. All identify fold-local augmentation and split-before-augment as the critical design features. ETD constraint enforced: definitive defense wins verdict.
all_issues_raised: augmentation-induced contamination concern — resolved by fold-local design

empirical_test.condition: Confirm split occurs before augmentation; confirm augmentation is generated only in training-fold training passes; confirm later-week test uses raw images; confirm part-level grouping at physical-unit granularity
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Augmentation occurred before fold assignment or augmented images appear in validation/test
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Pipeline confirms fold-local augmentation and split order; test is raw later-week data; no unit-level boundary crossing
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Augmentation is fold-local but part grouping is incomplete for some production batches
